meanings.definition: fasten or secure with a rope, string, or cord
examples: They tied their victim to the chair

meanings.definition: finish a game with an equal number of points, goals, etc.

examples: These big jets are tied to large airports

meanings.definition: connect, fasten, or put together two or more pieces
examples: Can you connect the two loudspeakers?

meanings.definition: form a knot or bow in

examples: The grandparents want to bond with the child
The minister married us on Saturday
We were wed the following week
The couple got spliced on Hawaii

meanings.definition: unite musical notes by a tie

examples: the guard was found trussed up with his arms and legs securely tied

meanings.definition: bound together by or as if by a strong rope; especially as by a bond of affection
examples: people tied by blood or marriage

meanings.definition: of the score in a contest
